tax_note: BTW (VAT) registration required; KOR scheme exempts VAT under €20K revenue; Box 1 progressive tax

vat_gst.name: BTW (Belasting over de Toegevoegde Waarde / VAT)
vat_gst.registration_threshold_note: The KOR scheme exempts small businesses under €20K from collecting and remitting BTW. Above €20K, BTW registration is mandatory. KOR participants cannot reclaim input BTW. Voluntary BTW registration is available at any level.
vat_gst.digital_products: Digital products sold to Dutch/EU consumers are subject to 21% BTW. Cross-border EU B2C sales use OSS. B2B sales within the EU use reverse charge (0% BTW on invoice, customer accounts for BTW).

digital_product_tax: Digital products are standard-rated at 21% BTW. The KOR exemption applies to digital product revenue. If selling across the EU, register for OSS to simplify cross-border VAT compliance. Ebooks are taxed at the reduced 9% rate.

entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Box 1 progressive tax (36.97-49.5%). Zelfstandigenaftrek (€3,750 deduction, being phased down) + MKB-winstvrijstelling (14% profit exemption). Effective rate can be quite low for modest profits.
entity_comparison.best_for: Starting out; freelancers; revenue under €100K; tax benefits via zelfstandigenaftrek

entity_comparison.liability: Limited to company assets (€0.01 minimum capital)
entity_comparison.tax_treatment: Vennootschapsbelasting: 19% on first €200K profit, 25.8% above. Director salary (DGA) subject to income tax. Dividends taxed at 26.9% (Box 2).
entity_comparison.best_for: Profit above €100K; liability protection; more tax-efficient at higher income levels; professional credibility
